title: Create an AI agent for options trading
author: roadmapsfyi
selftext:
Hi I'm looking to build an AI game on Claude/ openclaw for assisting me in options trading.
I'm curious if anyone has built anything similar?
Looking for suggestions from folks on what has worked and what has not worked for particularly options trading..
If you have any recommendations for using particular mcp tools ..pls share .
Thank you

comments.author: Spartacus_Nakamoto
comments.body:
Hey, I’m working on a project, it’s working.

https://old.reddit.com/r/ai_trading/comments/1sx57io/experimenting_with_llmbased_trading_via_alpaca/?utm_source=reddit&utm_medium=usertext&utm_name=CryptoTradingBot&utm_content=t1_oiyfidy

Biggest piece of advice is be careful with bid/ask spreads paper trading vs live. Especially with options. You’ll think you have a system that works then get wrecked by spreads. 

DM, happy to share what I’ve learned. Really trying to find others working on this.

comments.author: BuildwithPublic
comments.body:
**Draft:**

Built something similar - few things that actually matter:

**Works:** Natural language rules in the system prompt hold up well for regime filtering (VIX gates, skew conditions, etc.). MCP execution loops are clean if you keep the tool surface small.

**Doesn't work:** Claude will hallucinate greeks if you don't pipe in live chain data. Also be explicit on leg direction for spreads - vague prompts cause misfires.

**State:** Claude has no memory between sessions. Pass your position state back in each time or it starts fresh.

For execution r/PublicApp  has a native MCP server -options, zero commissions, $0.10/contract rebate, works directly with Claude Desktop. That's what I'd use as the execution layer.

Happy to share prompt structure if you have a specific strategy in mind
